construct: Treats mathematical formalism as revealing physical reality vs. treats mathematical formalism as computational convenience
positive_pole: Takes mathematical formalisms seriously as clues to deeper physical truths, even when they contradict established consensus
negative_pole: Uses mathematical tools pragmatically without inferring ontological implications about the nature of reality
behavioral_implication: When encountering a mathematically elegant but empirically unverified theory, this person would pursue its physical implications rather than treating it as a useful fiction

construct: Seeks theoretical unification through first principles vs. builds incrementally from experimental observations
positive_pole: Derives predictions from fundamental theoretical principles that can then be tested against reality
negative_pole: Accumulates experimental data and builds theories incrementally to fit observed patterns
behavioral_implication: Faced with unexplained phenomena, this person would construct theoretical frameworks to predict new observations rather than collecting more data to find empirical patterns

construct: Prioritizes intellectual authenticity over institutional advancement vs. adapts intellectual agenda to career requirements
positive_pole: Maintains independent research direction based on personal conviction about fundamental questions
negative_pole: Shapes research priorities and public positions to optimize professional standing and advancement
behavioral_implication: When facing career pressure to pursue fashionable research topics, this person would continue working on problems they consider fundamentally important regardless of professional consequences

construct: Treats persistent contradictions as signals for paradigm replacement vs. treats contradictions as technical problems requiring solution
positive_pole: Interprets systematic failures as indicating flawed foundational assumptions requiring conceptual revolution
negative_pole: Responds to theoretical problems by developing more sophisticated techniques within existing frameworks
behavioral_implication: When established theories generate persistent anomalies, this person would question fundamental assumptions rather than develop ad hoc modifications to preserve the existing paradigm

construct: Uses aesthetic judgment as guide to theoretical validity vs. evaluates theories primarily through empirical success
positive_pole: Treats mathematical beauty and conceptual elegance as reliable indicators of fundamental truth
negative_pole: Judges theoretical frameworks primarily by their predictive accuracy and practical utility
behavioral_implication: When comparing competing theories with similar empirical performance, this person would choose the one with greater mathematical elegance and conceptual unity

construct: Defends creative imagination as primary driver of discovery vs. emphasizes methodical knowledge accumulation
positive_pole: Views conceptual leaps and thought experiments as the fundamental source of scientific breakthrough
negative_pole: Relies on systematic observation and incremental data collection as the foundation of scientific progress
behavioral_implication: When training young scientists, this person would emphasize developing intuitive thinking and conceptual creativity over mastering experimental techniques

construct: Maintains theoretical consistency despite community consensus vs. adapts positions to align with scientific orthodoxy
positive_pole: Preserves core philosophical commitments even when isolated from mainstream scientific opinion
negative_pole: Adjusts theoretical positions to maintain credibility and connection with prevailing scientific consensus
behavioral_implication: When a new theory gains widespread acceptance but conflicts with fundamental philosophical beliefs, this person would continue developing alternative approaches rather than joining the consensus

construct: Gracefully concedes tactical arguments while preserving strategic positions vs. defends all positions with equal intensity
positive_pole: Distinguishes between specific technical claims and broader theoretical principles, willing to abandon the former to strengthen the latter
negative_pole: Treats all intellectual positions as equally fundamental, defending specific arguments with the same intensity as core principles
behavioral_implication: When presented with a valid refutation of a specific argument, this person would acknowledge the error publicly while repositioning their broader critique on more solid ground

construct: Integrates scientific knowledge with moral responsibility vs. maintains separation between technical knowledge and ethical concerns
positive_pole: Views scientific understanding as inherently creating moral agency and ethical obligations
negative_pole: Treats scientific knowledge as morally neutral, separating technical expertise from ethical judgment
behavioral_implication: When developing technology with potential harmful applications, this person would consider ethical implications as integral to the scientific work rather than someone else's responsibility

construct: Optimizes cognitive processes for breakthrough insights vs. conforms thinking patterns to social expectations
positive_pole: Cultivates and protects unconventional cognitive approaches that enable original discoveries
negative_pole: Adapts thinking processes to match conventional academic discourse and social norms
behavioral_implication: In collaborative environments, this person would structure interactions to preserve their unique thinking processes rather than fully adapting to group cognitive norms

construct: Evaluates roles functionally based on capability match vs. accepts prestigious positions based on honor or duty
positive_pole: Assesses opportunities by analyzing whether personal strengths align with role requirements for effectiveness
negative_pole: Makes decisions about positions and responsibilities based on symbolic importance and social recognition
behavioral_implication: When offered high-status positions outside their area of expertise, this person would decline if they believe others would be more effective, regardless of the prestige involved

construct: Commits to long-term theoretical projects despite uncertainty vs. diversifies efforts across multiple tractable problems
positive_pole: Sustains decades-long focus on fundamental questions even during periods of stagnation and isolation
negative_pole: Shifts research attention to areas with clearer progress markers and community engagement
behavioral_implication: When facing a potentially revolutionary but highly uncertain theoretical problem, this person would commit years of exclusive effort rather than hedging with parallel projects
